116571.explanation: The advantage is that residents become more directly involved in decision-making; the disadvantage is that many problems cannot be answered with a simple yes or no. Because municipal referendums are very costly, we will only use this means in exceptional cases.

116574.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs can easily coordinate opening hours among themselves.

116575.explanation: We offer shelter to people fleeing war, violence, or hunger.

116583.explanation: The municipality is responsible for public spaces; the entrepreneur for their own building. Therefore, we do not contribute to any costs.

116584.explanation: Yes, we support this with the City Pass.

116591.explanation: Camera surveillance has a temporary effect but is not a real solution. Therefore, we prefer to spend money on safety on prevention and addressing locations perceived as unsafe.

116593.explanation: Weapons make risky situations more dangerous.

116594.explanation: There are also people from other municipalities who do not yet live here but do work or go to school here.

116600.explanation: The municipality is here to help people, not to cause them problems.

116601.explanation: Currently, it is 120% of the social assistance norm. We think this can go to 125%.

116603.explanation: In cooperation with the national government.

116604.explanation: It is important that we encourage electric driving.

116610.explanation: You control your own faith, culture, or beliefs, not those of your caregiver.

116611.explanation: We must collaborate with organizations that provide the best care.

116613.explanation: Only if it is necessary to maintain facilities such as swimming pools and the library.

116616.explanation: There is much vacancy on existing sites and still enough space in Zevenhuis. Space in Hoorn is scarce and urgently needed for affordable housing and public green spaces.

116618.explanation: If people are allowed to stay here and receive a residence permit, they must also have somewhere to live. We reserve a small number of homes per year for this.

116620.explanation: There is no agricultural land in our municipality.

116627.explanation: Yes, we address the housing shortage by building upwards where possible.

116629.explanation: We are happy with our community centers and want to preserve them.

116630.explanation: We encourage parking at the city's edge, making our city center low in car traffic.

116631.explanation: Where new housing is added, an appropriate parking standard will apply.

116633.explanation: The statue of a mass murderer does not belong in our central square. As a compromise, we want to move the statue to the public garden of the West-Fries Museum, so the museum can provide context and explanation.

116634.explanation: A decision has already been made here.

116635.explanation: We want a low-traffic city center. There will remain spaces for city center residents and delivery to shops and hospitality must stay possible.

116636.explanation: Yes, electric vans provide easy transport for those who need it.

116637.explanation: The city beach is newly established and offers plenty of recreational opportunities. In the future, a kitesurfing location could be considered, but further west and not on the city beach.

116571.explanation: Hoorn Centraal wants to genuinely give residents influence over important decisions. For significant or sensitive issues, an advisory referendum can be a suitable tool to clarify residents’ wishes, as a supplement to good participation. We would like to explore whether this is possible within the municipality of Hoorn.

116574.explanation: We stand for entrepreneurship and livability. Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to determine their opening hours themselves, within clear and enforceable frameworks that prevent nuisance for neighbors and ensure a safe, vibrant city.

116575.explanation: We recognize the legal task of municipalities in accommodating refugees and displaced persons, but find an asylum seekers’ center within Hoorn inappropriate due to limited space and existing vulnerabilities in certain neighborhoods. We support a regional approach within West-Friesland for such a location.

116578.explanation: Allowing fireplaces and wood stoves respects freedom of choice and existing living situations. At the same time, the municipality can promote conscious use and cleaner alternatives, creating a balance between comfort, tradition, and health.

116583.explanation: Entrepreneurs want to make their premises accessible, but often lack the resources to make costly adaptations. Through financial support from the municipality, these barriers can be removed, which promotes inclusion and expands their customer base.

116584.explanation: Sports are essential for both physical and mental health, but costs are often a barrier for people with low incomes. By reimbursing memberships, the municipality promotes inclusivity, social participation, and a healthy lifestyle for everyone.

116591.explanation: We support targeted use of camera surveillance at vulnerable locations and favor hybrid, movable cameras so deployment can be flexible. It is important that camera surveillance is effectively monitored; additionally, privacy considerations must be carefully weighed.

116593.explanation: Equipping municipal enforcement officers (BOA’S) in Hoorn with batons can enhance their enforcement capacity and personal safety in potentially dangerous situations, but requires clear protocols, training, and consideration of societal impact.

116594.explanation: We advocate research into priority access to new-build homes for residents with a strong social and economic connection to Hoorn to strengthen local social cohesion, since this is hardly feasible for existing homes.

116597.explanation: Vacant offices can contribute to additional housing, but their number is limited and does not solve the shortage alone. Therefore, Hoorn Centraal also pursues other solutions like shared housing and landlord rentals to create living space more quickly.

116600.explanation: This prevents people on minimal incomes (social assistance level) from getting into financial trouble due to unaffordable levies and helps prevent distressing situations and debt problems, which can ultimately save the municipality money.

116601.explanation: We support using 120% of the minimum income as a threshold for eligibility, so residents in this group can also benefit from municipal assistance.

116603.explanation: A basic income has both advantages and disadvantages. It is important that implementing a universal basic income is financially feasible for everyone and that no exclusion occurs within groups entitled to it.

116604.explanation: There is a growing need for more charging points as more residents drive hybrid or electric. At the same time, we see the electrical grid is overloaded and it comes at the expense of regular parking spaces.

116610.explanation: We value personal freedom of choice. At the same time, appropriate care is not always available, leading to waiting lists or inaccessible services. Additionally, it can make care more expensive due to increased complexity.

116611.explanation: We believe that quality should be an important criterion in procurement. If the organization provides quality at a relatively low price, that is a bonus. We think price should never be the leading factor in the choice.

116613.explanation: We believe an increase in the property tax (OZB) can only be considered if necessary to preserve essential services. Tax increases must be carefully weighed and only implemented when there is a clear societal benefit.

116616.explanation: Expansion is often a necessary response to growing demand for space, but must be approached smartly by choosing redevelopment and quality over uncontrolled growth of the total number of business parks.

116618.explanation: We use the current urgency ordinance as a starting point but believe it should be reassessed in the next council period. The allocation of scarce social rental homes must remain fair and fit the capacity and needs of Blokker, Hoorn, and Zwaag.

116620.explanation: We want to protect agricultural land and seek sustainable alternatives (such as 'solar panels on roofs').

116625.explanation: We believe a coffee shop should be allowed, provided it does not cause nuisance to residents and illegal activities are strictly controlled.

116627.explanation: The municipal council has adopted a high-rise vision stating that a building may not exceed eight residential floors. Where possible, deviations can be allowed.

116629.explanation: We believe community centers are an important part of the neighborhood. Each neighborhood should have a community center where activities are organized to promote social cohesion.

116630.explanation: We are open to exploring whether parking fees can be lowered, but only if financially feasible for the municipality. We definitely want to prevent further increases in rates.

116631.explanation: We believe that the number of parking spaces should be assessed per neighborhood and actions taken accordingly.

116633.explanation: We believe historical images and monuments should remain visible as part of the city’s collective past. The statue of J.P. Coen is part of this. Explanation, context, and education belong with it; we must learn from the past and keep it as an educational topic.

116634.explanation: On May 13, 2025, it was decided that Hollandia will remain at Julianapark.

116635.explanation: A fully car-free Kerkplein is not necessary. We advocate a low-traffic design that ensures safety while maintaining accessibility for entrepreneurs, residents, and emergency services.

116636.explanation: To improve accessibility and livability of the city center, the municipality could consider deploying shuttle buses between parking garages and the center, so both visitors and residents have easier and more sustainable access to facilities.

116637.explanation: The city beach is a scarce public space for everyone. A new watersport location would put too much pressure on nature, tranquility, and accessibility. Hoorn Centraal chooses to preserve balance and quality.

116571.explanation: We attach great importance to citizen participation, but believe this does not always have to be through a referendum. We advocate for a stronger participation process in which residents are involved in decision-making from the start.

116574.explanation: Opening hours are up to the operator. The current system with closing times works well.

116575.explanation: An asylum seekers' center requires a large-scale location: under the dispersion law, Hoorn must provide shelter for 400 asylum seekers. Hoorn Lokaal cooperates with small-scale emergency reception, but our densely populated city has no space for these large numbers. Hoorn Lokaal wants the national government to ensure a lower influx of asylum seekers.

116578.explanation: Wood stoves may only be used if they do not cause nuisance to neighbors. Health and quality of life come first. The municipality must be able to take action against structural smoke and odor nuisance and protect residents from harmful air pollution.

116583.explanation: Accessibility of buildings is very important; everyone must be able to reach their workplace or institution easily. Entrepreneurs are obliged to make buildings accessible to all. Subsidies can be made available to make monumental (business) buildings accessible.

116584.explanation: It is important that everyone, especially children, can participate in sports activities. The existing scheme for people with low income must be maintained.

116591.explanation: Hoorn Lokaal believes that camera surveillance has become indispensable in our society. It prevents nuisance and crime and helps with investigations. In short: it contributes to a safer environment, and that weighs very heavily for Hoorn Lokaal!

116593.explanation: Community officers increasingly find themselves in difficult situations. A baton can sometimes provide a solution.

116594.explanation: The waiting lists for social rental housing are long, especially for young people and starters. Housing seekers from Hoorn should be given priority over those from other West Frisian municipalities as long as these municipalities do not build enough homes themselves.

116597.explanation: Vacant offices can contribute to additional housing, but their number is limited and does not solve the shortage alone. Therefore, Hoorn Lokaal also focuses on other solutions, such as shared housing and subletting by hosts, to create living space faster.

116600.explanation: Debt forgiveness is part of the policy of the municipality of Hoorn to financially support vulnerable residents. Hoorn Lokaal does not want to alter this.

116601.explanation: The income limit for aid with poverty currently exceeds the social assistance standard, namely up to 120%.

116603.explanation: Several cities in the Netherlands are conducting a pilot with a discretionary allowance for residents with social assistance benefits. Hoorn Lokaal would like to give such a pilot a chance.

116604.explanation: There are already many designated places in Hoorn for charging electric cars. That currently suffices; no additional spots are needed yet.

116610.explanation: It is important that the municipality of Hoorn ensures sufficient offerings for everyone, not so much specific provisions based on background or beliefs.

116611.explanation: Now that the municipality has taken control over youth care, we are gaining more insight into the issues facing our youth, as well as visibility into the quality and costs of youth care providers. We must be sharp on both quality and price.

116613.explanation: The municipality must pursue good and solid financial policies and keep the burden on residents as low as possible. An increase of the property tax (ozb) is not on the agenda.

116616.explanation: There is very little space available in Hoorn, while many companies want to expand or settle here. The region also lacks space for (new) businesses. Hoorn Lokaal wants Zevenhuis to be developed quickly.

116618.explanation: Hoorn Lokaal respects the current urgency regulation but believes it should be reassessed in the new council period. The distribution of scarce social rental homes must remain fair and fit the capacity and needs of Hoorn.

116620.explanation: Hoorn has no land available for this purpose.

116625.explanation: Hoorn currently has two coffeeshops, which is sufficient. New establishments are not desirable.

116627.explanation: High-rise buildings must fit the scale of Hoorn and also fit into the environment. For tall residential towers, we envision buildings over fifteen floors, for which there is little space in Hoorn. Building higher in some places helps against the housing shortage and is certainly open for discussion.

116629.explanation: It is important that residents in Hoorn have a place to meet each other and where care and social assistance can be offered.

116630.explanation: At the edge of the center, just a two-minute walk away, parking costs have already been lowered to a maximum of €5.50 per day. Hoorn Lokaal chooses an attractive and accessible city center with a good balance between affordability, traffic flow, and livability.

116631.explanation: Space in Hoorn is very scarce. New parking spaces may not come at the expense of green areas or livability.

116633.explanation: Hoorn Lokaal is open to good discussions on how to deal with J.P. Coen. We welcome that conversations about the past are being held in the present; we must continue this respectfully.

116634.explanation: Football club Hollandia will remain in Julianapark, as recently decided by the council.

116635.explanation: The Kerkplein does not need to be completely car-free. Hoorn Lokaal opts for a car-reduced design with space for safety and good accessibility for entrepreneurs, residents, and emergency services.

116636.explanation: The walking distance from the Jeudje and Parkschouwburg parking garages to the center is minimal; shuttle buses are not necessary.

116637.explanation: Creating a new watersports location at the city beach can contribute to both recreational opportunities for residents and tourist appeal, provided there is sufficient attention to safety, the environment, and the interests of other users.

116571.explanation: EenHoorn stands for open and transparent politics. Let residents decide together on major issues. That's only fair.

116574.explanation: EenHoorn stands for less regulatory pressure. The hospitality sector can regulate this perfectly well itself.

116575.explanation: The dispersion law alone does not provide solutions for the integration of asylum seekers. Municipalities face a housing shortage with long waiting lists that keep getting longer. The government in The Hague must ensure the problem is tackled as a whole.

116578.explanation: Of course, smoke nuisance must be countered as much as possible. We prefer to encourage solutions rather than impose bans.

116583.explanation: Financial assistance is a broad concept. Here too, we base ourselves on the principle that stimulating, for example with a subsidy or loan, is better than imposing requirements.

116584.explanation: EenHoorn believes that social assistance recipients should be helped to participate fully in society. Taking part in social life through clubs builds self-confidence to engage fully in society.

116591.explanation: EenHoorn supports more police presence on the streets. From a preventive view, there should be more camera surveillance at places of vandalism and nuisance.

116593.explanation: Respect for emergency workers is declining more and more. EenHoorn believes this must be tackled strongly. For both preventive reasons and the safety of community officers, carrying a baton is a good instrument.

116594.explanation: EenHoorn believes it is time to put our own residents first again. Solving the very long waiting list for social rental housing should be an absolute priority for Hoorn residents.

116597.explanation: The municipality may of course encourage property owners with certain tools. Whether this always has to be with money we doubt. A quick procedure, less regulation, and speedy processing of permit applications seem more effective to us.

116600.explanation: EenHoorn sees no purpose whatsoever in this statement.

116601.explanation: EenHoorn stands for tailored assistance. A fixed income limit provides clarity, but there must always be room for customization due to unforeseen circumstances, for example.

116603.explanation: EenHoorn supports the basic income as long as it does not disadvantage residents. We particularly see opportunities to reduce the jungle of rules and benefits.

116604.explanation: For EenHoorn, sustainability is not an end in itself. Needs and facilities must continue to align. One does not have to come at the expense of the other.

116610.explanation: For EenHoorn, care comes first. No requirements should be attached to that. Waiting lists for care are already generally too long.

116611.explanation: EenHoorn believes that youth care procurement should always be based on needs. Additionally, you achieve more with quality. Cuts should never be made on aid for children in need.

116613.explanation: EenHoorn believes that the municipality should always first make internal cuts if there is a budget shortfall somewhere.

116616.explanation: Where it stimulates employment and demonstrates the usefulness and necessity of the expansion, EenHoorn supports attracting more businesses to our city.

116618.explanation: EenHoorn is absolutely opposed to the policy from The Hague. The refugee problem is being dumped over the fence to the municipalities without help or policy. Refugees are placed in vulnerable neighborhoods with social rental housing. The current policy is bleak and unfair.

116620.explanation: EenHoorn believes you should work as much as possible from opportunities and possibilities. Prohibitions do not fit in our way of thinking. Think in terms of utility and necessity.

116625.explanation: Coffeeshops largely combat illegal street trade and the sale of dangerous drugs. The rules in Hoorn are strict, and therefore the nuisance around the shops is minimal.

116627.explanation: The housing shortage is increasing more and more. One solution is to build higher where possible. There are beautiful examples in cities with wonderful architecture and greenery. Apartment buildings are not just square blocks. Make something beautiful of them.

116629.explanation: EenHoorn believes neighborhood centers are indispensable for all districts. It is becoming increasingly important to organize various activities for residents from a social perspective. Neighborhood centers can also play an informative role.

116630.explanation: Across the board, costs are rising for everyone. Making parking more expensive will not make visiting or staying longer in the city center more attractive. Certainly not for tourists or visitors from the region. The competition is great regionally.

116631.explanation: EenHoorn believes you should facilitate according to need. With too few parking spaces, the problem is just shifted to other neighborhoods. Also ensure good public transport or other alternatives. Stimulate according to need.

116633.explanation: The statue is the whetstone of our city and publicly reveals the good and bad sides of our city, which is rich in VOC history. Hiding the statue changes nothing about this.

116634.explanation: EenHoorn believes every neighborhood has the right to a sports club. Additionally, we oppose any form of coercion to make clubs relocate or merge.

116635.explanation: EenHoorn believes that the Church Square and its surroundings should remain accessible to car traffic. Closing streets causes a ripple effect elsewhere and makes supplying shops and businesses difficult. Possibly from 4 pm during the summer months.

116636.explanation: As far as applicable in Hoorn, EenHoorn believes this fits within a broader policy: low car traffic where possible and accessibility where necessary.

116637.explanation: We wholeheartedly welcome every activity or event. But as stated earlier, we see no responsibility to finance them. We will facilitate through quick permitting and procedures.

116571.explanation: Residents must have direct say in major decisions. That increases trust and involvement in local government.

116574.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best what works themselves, within clear frameworks for safety and nuisance.

116575.explanation: Hoorn is already under great pressure regarding housing, care, and safety and cannot bear more.

116578.explanation: A general ban is disproportionate; nuisance must be addressed in a targeted manner.

116583.explanation: Accessibility is important, but support must remain targeted and affordable.

116584.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in sports; this prevents social exclusion.

116591.explanation: Camera surveillance helps in preventing and detecting crime and nuisance.

116593.explanation: Community enforcement officers must be able to protect themselves during escalations.

116594.explanation: Residents of Hoorn must not be left behind in their own municipality.

116597.explanation: Vacant offices offer opportunities to address the housing shortage and should be actively utilized.

116600.explanation: Protection of low incomes remains necessary.

116601.explanation: We choose tailored solutions instead of constantly raising or closing fixed limits. Assistance must be focused on people's situations, not just a number.

116603.explanation: Too expensive and insufficiently proven to be effective.

116604.explanation: Expansion where needed, but not at the expense of parking pressure.

116610.explanation: Freedom of choice increases trust and leads to appropriate care.

116616.explanation: Employment and economic growth are crucial for Hoorn.

116618.explanation: That comes at the expense of our own residents.

116620.explanation: Protection of the landscape and food production is paramount.

116625.explanation: A regulated coffee shop ensures clarity and safety, provided it is properly enforced.

116627.explanation: High-rise buildings can be allowed in specific locations, but are not desirable everywhere.

116629.explanation: Neighborhood centers strengthen social cohesion and community interaction.

116630.explanation: Improves accessibility and appeal of the city center.

116631.explanation: The current parking pressure is too high.

116633.explanation: History should be explained, not erased.

116634.explanation: Sports facilities are just as important as green spaces.

116635.explanation: Accessibility and usage must be the guiding principles.

116636.explanation: Only meaningful when there is a demonstrable need.

116637.explanation: A watersports location, for example for kitesurfing, suits Hoorn as a water city.

116571.explanation: Referendums increase and sharpen divisions within society. Complex social issues cannot be reduced to a simple yes/no question.

116574.explanation: Hospitality is important for the vibrancy of the city but must be balanced with the livability and safety of the surroundings.

116575.explanation: We comply with legal provisions. The reception of asylum seekers requires a tailored approach. Support among our residents is considered for each location.

116578.explanation: We are not against the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves, but emphasize responsible, healthy, and sustainable use.

116583.explanation: We stand for an inclusive and accessible public space. For businesses, the responsibility lies with the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can provide encouraging advice.

116584.explanation: Sports are not only healthy but also connect people. We want to continue the Hoornpas, which enables residents with low incomes to participate in sports.

116591.explanation: Flexible cameras can be deployed in areas with a lot of nuisance.

116593.explanation: CDA Hoorn wants the trial allowing enforcement officers to carry batons to be continued.

116594.explanation: We want priority to be given to residents of Hoorn and West-Friesland who have an economic or historical connection, or who provide informal care.

116597.explanation: Converting vacant offices can contribute to more housing. The municipality supports where necessary, but implementation lies with the owners and developers.

116600.explanation: Especially in times of inflation, housing shortages, high energy costs, and uncertainty, the municipality must stand firmly for vulnerable residents.

116601.explanation: We advocate for careful spending of tax money so that we can continue investing in poverty reduction, social safety nets, and provisions for those who truly need them.

116603.explanation: We advocate for the introduction of the basic job: a paid position for people who have difficulty finding regular employment. This promotes labor participation and offers people a perspective on meaningful societal participation.

116604.explanation: We want to encourage the use of sustainable technologies.

116610.explanation: The CDA wants care that aligns with personal wishes and background but believes 'always' is not feasible. What matters is a system that inspires trust, reduces bureaucracy, and makes care more person-centered so that choices can be realized as much as possible.

116611.explanation: CDA Hoorn chooses trust in the healthcare provider, stability, long-term contracts, and clear agreements between the municipality and care providers.

116613.explanation: Raising taxes is not taken lightly; we only increase annually by the inflation rate. An increase in municipal taxes is only possible when the provisions for our residents are at stake.

116616.explanation: CDA Hoorn values strong and vital business parks.

116618.explanation: We follow the urgency regulation according to the nationally applicable housing law.

116620.explanation: We prefer an experiment with solar panels above parking spaces.

116625.explanation: CDA Hoorn continues to commit to a strong preventive approach. Preventing alcohol, drug use, and vaping is part of this.

116627.explanation: When building more apartments, we focus on 'gentle density' to create pleasant neighborhoods that suit our city. High-rise buildings offer few benefits and do not fit with the community spirit we want to preserve in Hoorn. We maintain the balance between housing and green space.

116629.explanation: Investing in local facilities, such as neighborhood centers, is self-evident to us. It increases the livability of the district.

116630.explanation: The current parking management helps keep the city center safe, clear, and livable.

116631.explanation: We continue to focus on sufficient parking for residents. Besides car parking spaces, we also emphasize enough parking for bicycles.

116633.explanation: We believe more context and education should be provided about the J.P. Coen statue.

116634.explanation: Julianapark deserves more greenery. By relocating the Hollandia fields, more green can be added to the park, creating even more space for recreation and green areas.

116635.explanation: We support a car-reduced city center, while maintaining parking options for disabled and accessible parking.

116636.explanation: Shuttle buses to the center make Hoorn accessible to everyone, encourage visits to shops and museums, and thus contribute to a vibrant and inviting city.

116637.explanation: If there are plans for developing a watersports location, it must be done in collaboration with the water authority and the province. The Markermeer is a protected natural area.

116571.explanation: We are not in favor of a referendum. We consciously choose to engage in daily conversations with residents to gather what is on their minds and happening in the community.

116574.explanation: We want to leave it to the hospitality industry, but within legal limits.

116575.explanation: There is no room for an asylum seekers' center in Hoorn.

116578.explanation: We believe that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed. We choose freedom and personal responsibility.

116583.explanation: 25% of the residents have a disability. This is therefore necessary.

116584.explanation: We understand the intention behind this form of support but have concerns about how it is perceived by people who work and cannot afford a sports club themselves. Therefore, we do not consider this an appropriate and responsible form of assistance.

116591.explanation: Camera surveillance is important to record violations, of course with respect for privacy.

116593.explanation: A baton is sometimes necessary for a public order officer (boa) to properly carry out their work.

116594.explanation: There is a housing shortage in Hoorn, so we believe that Hoorn residents should be given priority.

116597.explanation: We want vacant offices to be converted, but this should be funded by a municipal subsidy. After all, our residents would then contribute financially. We do not want that.

116600.explanation: We believe taxes should be as low as possible, but also that taxes should not be written off too easily.

116601.explanation: We do not want to raise the income limit.

116603.explanation: In our opinion, work must pay off. A universal basic income will not contribute to that. Especially not if it leads to higher taxes. So, we are also against a trial.

116604.explanation: We think there are currently too few places to charge electric cars.

116610.explanation: We believe there should be no choice restriction for qualified care providers. Every qualified care provider should be able to help the client.

116611.explanation: No, also regarding quality and efficiency.

116613.explanation: Property tax (ozb) should actually be lowered.

116616.explanation: This is how we invest in our economy and jobs.

116618.explanation: No, for us everyone is equal.

116620.explanation: We are not in favor of banning it; it actually generates energy.

116625.explanation: We want to allow this. There is already room for it in the municipality and these already exist.

116627.explanation: The VVD is in favor of high-rise buildings where possible.

116629.explanation: A neighborhood center is a great place to come together.

116630.explanation: In our view, paid parking should become cheaper.

116631.explanation: We strive for more parking spaces in neighborhoods.

116633.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the statue should remain standing.

116634.explanation: We believe that football clubs should be able to use their football fields unchanged.

116635.explanation: The Church Square does not need to be car-free in our opinion.

116636.explanation: For those who want to park outside the center and have limited mobility, a shuttle should be available. This way we want to keep access from the outskirts to the city center accessible for everyone.

116637.explanation: We think it is a good plan to build a new watersports location at the city beach.

116571.explanation: D66 supports other forms of citizen participation, such as neighborhood labs and neighborhood budgets.

116574.explanation: As a liberal party with heart for the Hoorn entrepreneur, we believe that hospitality businesses should have as much freedom as possible over their opening times.

116575.explanation: D66 supports compliance with the distribution law. That means we must also contribute our part in Hoorn.

116578.explanation: We are not in favor of wood burning but believe banning it goes too far.

116583.explanation: We encourage entrepreneurs to make their buildings more accessible, but they should bear those costs themselves.

116584.explanation: D66 supports increasing the 'sports pass' (participation pass), which allows people on social assistance benefits to partially pay for memberships, among other things.

116591.explanation: We support the use of flexible cameras, but fixed camera surveillance does not contribute to safety.

116593.explanation: Our public order officers already have batons because we participate in a national pilot.

116594.explanation: In principle, we are not in favor of this, but we believe that people with certain professions such as teachers, doctors, or police officers should be allowed priority.

116597.explanation: Developers develop houses, the municipality does not. However, we may play a role in sustainability efforts.

116600.explanation: People are responsible for their own debts, but helping people further into debt has counterproductive effects.

116601.explanation: Hoorn already applies a generous 120% of the social assistance amount as a standard for this.

116603.explanation: We do not want this at the municipal level; it is up to the national government to decide on this or not.

116604.explanation: We would like more charging points, but first a solution must be found for the national electricity problem.

116610.explanation: Care providers deliver care; religious beliefs do not play a role in this.

116611.explanation: Care must remain affordable, but the quality of care is paramount.

116613.explanation: Taxes are not an end in themselves. If municipal services come under pressure, increasing the property tax (ozb) is worth considering.

116616.explanation: There is a great need for this; D66 wants to stimulate local entrepreneurship.

116618.explanation: D66 Hoorn wants us to comply with national laws and regulations, and this should not be a political issue at the municipal level.

116620.explanation: We do not want to ban this. By the way, we hardly have any agricultural land.

116625.explanation: We already have a coffeeshop; D66 would like to continue allowing it. However, we would like to see decent national policy on drugs.

116627.explanation: To save space (for example, for more greenery), we can build somewhat higher in certain places, for instance up to eight stories.

116629.explanation: We want to invest in, for example, bringing libraries back to the neighborhoods.

116630.explanation: To make the city center car-restricted and thus more pleasant to stay in, we want to make parking on the outskirts of the city cheaper and encourage it.

116631.explanation: In Hoorn, Blokker, and Zwaag there is simply no space for more parking spots at street level. We prefer to use scarce space for greenery and housing.

116633.explanation: D66 believes that the statue of J.P. Coen in its current form is untenable. Relocation is one of many options.

116634.explanation: Hollandia has more football fields than needed. Especially at this location, it makes sense to return part of it to the city.

116635.explanation: The pilot with a car-restricted Kerkplein is considered successful by D66. Complete car-free status is not possible due to accessibility.

116636.explanation: The municipality is not a transport company, but we welcome initiatives that encourage people to park on the outskirts of the city instead of inside it.

116637.explanation: The current city beach has (so far) proven unsuitable for this. In 2027, this will be reconsidered by the water authority. We are very pleased with the way the beach was established and is being used.

116571.explanation: We are not in favor of referenda.

116574.explanation: In consultation with hospitality entrepreneurs and residents, we support extending closing times for hospitality businesses including cafés, restaurants, and snack bars.

116575.explanation: We have an emergency shelter location. Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer voted against it.

116578.explanation: Not an issue in the municipal council.

116583.explanation: We want Hoorn to become a wheelchair-friendly municipality. We want adjustments at the viaduct over the Provincial Road between Kersenboogerd (Hunzeweg) and Venenlaankwartier (Holenweg) so that users of wheelchairs, mobility scooters, and walkers can use the viaduct.

116584.explanation: We do this only for the youth. Adults receive a card with an amount to spend.

116591.explanation: We continue to find camera surveillance important in the city center and near the Kersenboogerd station area. Camera surveillance by Park Management in business parks is a good example and has increased safety.

116593.explanation: Many citizens are annoyed by nuisance caused by petty crime, noisy mopeds, illegal parking, cycling in shopping centers, arson, and (car) break-ins. The municipality’s enforcement officers (BOAs) must take action against this and be given more resources to do so.

116594.explanation: Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer wants housing seekers with economic ties to the municipality and the West Friesland region to be given priority.

116597.explanation: We want suitable youth housing and student accommodation to be realized in Hoorn. More construction of homes for elderly people with care is necessary. We encourage other forms of housing for the elderly, such as the construction of age-friendly communities (Knarrenhofjes), nursing homes, and care homes.

116600.explanation: Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer believes in a broad approach to combating poverty. Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer wants a generous budget for poverty reduction. We want room for new initiatives, including experiments to combat poverty.

116601.explanation: We stand up for working people with low incomes. We want children not to grow up in poverty and for society to do everything possible to reach the group in need. We want children of working parents with low incomes to qualify.

116603.explanation: Thanks to the Hoornpas, everyone, young and old, can participate in activities of sports, arts, and cultural associations. We encourage that no one is excluded from participation through circumstances beyond their control. Efforts must be made to increase awareness of these arrangements.

116604.explanation: The purchase of electric cars will increase. Therefore, more charging points must be installed. We promote the realization of a fast-charging station.

116610.explanation: Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer believes that everyone who needs care should receive it. We find access to care important for everyone, regardless of income or age. We want accessible care for all residents who need it and more preventive care in general.

116611.explanation: Repeatedly contracting youth care to different organizations is undesirable. The national government should give more funding to municipalities for better youth care. Constantly re-tendering youth care to other organizations does not always improve quality.

116613.explanation: We are against unnecessary cost increases for residents and entrepreneurs. The property tax (ozb) will increase only with inflation (trend-based).

116616.explanation: This is not an issue within the municipality. We still have sufficient business park space.

116618.explanation: The mandatory integration of status holders (refugees with residence permits) is most successful by allocating housing to status holders dispersed throughout the city, offering Dutch language education, and providing a place in the labor market.

116620.explanation: We have no agricultural land. A good environment is everyone's responsibility. Through more greenery in gardens, solar panels, electric driving, buying locally, insulation, less waste, more reuse, and more trees, we help the environment and ourselves.

116625.explanation: The municipality of Hoorn already has a coffee shop.

116627.explanation: Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer wants high-rise buildings only where possible. These high-rise locations can only be in areas where residents do not experience nuisance.

116629.explanation: This is the current policy and we support it.

116630.explanation: Despite the construction of an underground parking garage at the P&R site, we find it necessary that paid parking in the city center remains possible for livability and the economy. Shopkeepers cannot do without customers who come by car. The rate of €5.50 per hour is too high.

116631.explanation: We believe that residents benefit from sufficient parking spaces, good roads, and adequate, smart, and well-maintained cycling and walking paths.

116633.explanation: The statue of murderer and benefactor J.P. Coen remains on the Roode Steen. Not to glorify J.P. Coen’s deeds, but to not erase or hide the past. Removing the statue will not change the history about J.P. Coen. The text on the pedestal provides good information.

116634.explanation: Football club Hollandia remains with an additional artificial turf field in Julianapark. Hollandia and Julianapark strengthen each other.

116635.explanation: We want to continue the policy of short-term parking in the city center and long-term parking on the outskirts of the city center. A shuttle bus can help with this.

116636.explanation: We want to continue the policy of short-term parking in the city center and long-term parking on the outskirts of the city center. A shuttle bus can help with this.

116637.explanation: Sociaal Hoorn/Team Tonnaer would like the city beach to also become a challenging environment for exercise and sports.

116571.explanation: In difficult choices, it is important that the municipal council makes decisions. Referendums often cause confusion. A broad survey is then a better way to involve residents.

116574.explanation: It is good to keep an eye on this and implement policy, especially during special days like King's Day or Lappendag.

116575.explanation: There is no room for a permanent asylum seeker center in Hoorn. However, temporary emergency shelter locations are possible. We have a preference for an emergency shelter location along the A7 in the municipality of Koggenland. If a shelter location is established, it will be done openly, in consultation and cooperation with residents and neighbors.

116578.explanation: This should remain possible, although it must be considered whether an advisory can be given against burning in certain weather conditions.

116583.explanation: The municipality of Hoorn actively seeks ways for residents with disabilities to participate in sports. The ChristenUnie wants municipal buildings to be accessible to everyone. Digitally, the municipality must also be easily reachable for people with disabilities.

116584.explanation: In Hoorn, we have the sports and culture pass for families and people receiving social benefits.

116591.explanation: A safely designed outdoor space (such as parks, squares, and roads) that is co-designed from a woman's perspective. We also want to examine where, besides the physical space, camera surveillance can be used.

116593.explanation: There is currently a trial running. It is good to wait for the results. This is national legislation.

116594.explanation: This already happens via housing corporation Intermaris, which gives housing seekers from Hoorn and West Friesland priority.

116597.explanation: Where possible, facilitating the conversion of office buildings into homes is important. If necessary, subsidies can be granted for this, but that is not the immediate starting point.

116600.explanation: The municipality of Hoorn participates in the Debt Relief covenant of the NVVK. This is an agreement to better help people with debts and make arrangements with creditors.

116601.explanation: The municipality has a generous policy for people with a low income. The threshold of 120% of the minimum income remains the same.

116603.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports a trial with a basic income.

116604.explanation: If there is more need for this in neighborhoods, residents can apply for it. We want to facilitate demand more specifically.

116610.explanation: It would be nice if this is offered. In some situations, this will not be possible and there will not always be a choice. Also given the scarcity in the labor market in healthcare.

116611.explanation: Good care must be the starting point, not the price.

116613.explanation: The starting point is that the property tax (ozb) is not increased more than in line with trends, although the ChristenUnie is willing to increase it if vital facilities and care for our residents are at risk.

116616.explanation: Zevenhuis must be further developed. Hoorn 80 will eventually become a live-work environment. The other sites cannot expand further.

116618.explanation: The municipality provides housing for people who have received a residence permit (status holders). We work together with social organizations, such as churches, to help them feel at home. The municipality arranges accommodation and reimburses costs.

116620.explanation: In Hoorn we have little to no agricultural land.

116625.explanation: The ChristenUnie opposes the presence of coffee shops and/or the cultivation of cannabis by municipalities.

116627.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants more high-rise buildings around, for example, the provincial road and train stations, with a maximum height of eight to ten floors.

116629.explanation: Neighborhood centers are important for the districts. There is now a neighborhood center in each district. There must be no budget cuts here.

116630.explanation: Last year we set the rates, making it slightly more expensive in the center and cheaper on the outskirts to encourage this. Paid parking in the center does not need to become cheaper.

116631.explanation: At the moment, there are enough parking spaces. We must continue to evaluate whether there are sufficient places. This can be done well with the scanning car.

116633.explanation: The statue of J.P. Coen will not remain in its current form on the Roode Steen, but we will provide more explanation with the statue, move it to the entrance of the Westfries Museum, or update or add to the statue. As far as we are concerned, it should not disappear from public space.

116634.explanation: We just made a decision that it remains and that more park area will be added.

116635.explanation: Roode Steen and Kerkplein will, with the exception of some disabled parking spaces and short-term parking spots, become car-free.

116636.explanation: Everything is within walking distance. At most, a shuttle could run from the parking lot near the fire station. But I would do this in consultation with museums and entrepreneurs.

116571.explanation: An advisory referendum is an essential part of democracy, but we prefer policies that are supported by early and transparent dialogue with residents about plans.

116574.explanation: Of course, we gladly offer freedom to entrepreneurs, but having a means of enforcement ensures labor laws can be more easily observed. Additionally, it can prevent nuisance during the nights.

116575.explanation: Hoorn must act compassionately towards people fleeing war; maintaining an asylum seekers' center is a good example of this.

116578.explanation: The fine dust emitted by these stoves is not only harmful to the environment and air quality, but also affects the health of residents nearby. The municipality should assist with the switch to electric heating where needed.

116583.explanation: Accessibility promotes equality. This fits within the vision of the Party for the Animals. This is especially essential in an old city center like that of Hoorn.

116584.explanation: Sports are healthy; it should not be the case that someone lacks the means to adopt healthy behavior.

116591.explanation: Privacy is a great value. Camera surveillance compromises this without truly providing safety.

116593.explanation: A baton is absolutely unnecessary and even harmful to the role of an enforcer.

116594.explanation: This is unfeasible; social housing is regulated across West Friesland.

116597.explanation: Addressing vacancy can free up much housing space in the short term. The municipality must definitely prioritize this.

116600.explanation: Tax waivers help residents with low incomes keep their heads above water. We do not abandon these residents.

116601.explanation: Poverty is unhealthy and costly for the municipality; preventing it is an important task of the municipality.

116603.explanation: A basic income drastically reduces poverty and promotes greater equality in society. So, a good idea.

116604.explanation: This is a good step for the transition to electric transport. Furthermore, we advocate for fewer parking spaces in general and encourage public or shared transport.

116610.explanation: There is a shortage of care, so this is not always possible. Patients are free to express a preference, but a requirement is neither possible nor desirable for care institutions.

116611.explanation: We prioritize welfare over wealth, which means that price should never be the main selection criterion in care.

116613.explanation: To fund social policies, money must be raised where it is, so the property tax (ozb) may increase.

116616.explanation: The municipality is already densely built; expanding industrial areas goes hand in hand with reducing scarce green spaces. We should focus on transforming, for example, Hoorn 80 in the long term into a sustainable residential area.

116618.explanation: As legally required, the municipality must implement this. Moreover, it is our humane duty.

116620.explanation: Farmland should be converted into nature reserves to contribute to biodiversity. We do not want solar parks in natural areas.

116625.explanation: We already have several in Hoorn; the Party for the Animals sees no reason to deny an entrepreneur who wants to start a coffeeshop.

116627.explanation: To provide sufficient housing space for our residents, high-rise buildings are essential. We focus on accessible, sustainable, and green high-rises to keep the locations livable where this is realized.

116629.explanation: This is mostly already the case within Hoorn, and in our view should remain so. A community center in the neighborhood provides a social meeting point for residents.

116630.explanation: Our plan is for a green, car-free city center. It is therefore wrong to grant discounts for car traffic to park there.

116631.explanation: The municipality should focus on reducing the number of parking spaces by providing better bike paths and public transport connections. The freed parking spaces can then be transformed into, for example, wider sidewalks, children's play areas, or flower-rich verges.

116633.explanation: The infamous image of J.P. Coen should be removed from the Roode Steen and relocated to the museum garden.

116634.explanation: This is a fait accompli, but a good management plan for the area must be developed.

116635.explanation: The city center suffers from excessive paving, which is largely maintained for the sake of car traffic. Additionally, cars cause air pollution in the city center. It’s better to exclude them from the center and significantly increase greenery there.

116636.explanation: Our ideal vision includes a car-free city center with shuttle buses transporting people from transfer points outside to inside the city center. In an ideal situation, this is therefore a good investment.

116637.explanation: Hoorn has sufficient water sports locations. An additional location on the city beach is unnecessary in our view.

116571.explanation: Voting once every four years and influencing decisions in that way is too little. Residents must have continuous influence. Referenda can help but are often emergency brakes. We choose for constant involvement through neighborhood teams, advisory councils, and citizen assemblies. This way, people participate in decisions about their own neighborhood.

116574.explanation: The hospitality sector faces outdated rules like restrictive closing times. The SP wants to update hospitality policy and renew it together with entrepreneurs and residents, allowing more vibrancy and livability. More freedom in opening hours fits this approach.

116575.explanation: The SP stands for a dignified life without war and violence. The dispersion law prevents dire situations like in Ter Apel. That’s why we support fair, small-scale asylum reception with community support. We use existing real estate and invest in livability, safety, and agreements.

116578.explanation: For many residents, the wood stove is an affordable heat source and part of living pleasure. Banning it won’t solve air quality but mainly hits people’s wallets. The SP chooses education and cleaner burning, not unreasonable bans.

116583.explanation: The SP believes everyone should be able to fully participate. The municipality must help entrepreneurs make their buildings and services accessible: wheelchair-friendly entrances and passages, easily accessible public toilets, and services accessible without digital barriers for everyone.

116584.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone. Lack of money should not be a reason to be excluded. That’s why the municipality should reimburse sports club memberships for residents on social assistance, so health, social contact, and participation remain possible for all.

116591.explanation: Safety is not increased by more cameras. Cameras mainly infringe on privacy and work retrospectively. We choose real safety: more neighborhood police officers, better lighting, and prevention in neighborhoods.

116593.explanation: The SP opts for safety through prevention, visible neighborhood police, and proper enforcement, not through further arming. Additional weapons do not enhance de-escalation. Enforcement officers must remain professional and approachable; real safety arises through community work, supervision, and social measures.

116594.explanation: SP Hoorn wants at least 50% of new homes to be prioritized for housing seekers from Hoorn. This keeps affordable homes available for local residents and gives young people and families a fair chance at a home in their familiar environment.

116597.explanation: The SP wants to actively combat vacancy. In the housing crisis, it is unacceptable that offices and buildings remain unused for years. The municipality must stimulate repurposing, accelerate conversion to affordable housing, and prompt property owners to act.

116600.explanation: Waiving municipal taxes is a necessary social safety net for residents with low incomes. It prevents debt and poverty. Especially in difficult times, the municipality must protect vulnerable residents instead of taxing them more.

116601.explanation: Too many residents fall just outside eligibility while struggling to make ends meet because prices keep rising. The income threshold should be raised so more people qualify for minimum social provisions and debt assistance. This way we fight poverty earlier and more fairly.

116603.explanation: Besides a higher minimum wage and strong social security, the SP supports a pilot with basic income. This can be useful to reduce poverty. An important caveat is that benefits should not be lowered and security must be maintained. Fair wages will always remain the foundation for the SP.

116604.explanation: Not everyone can or wants to drive electric. Additional charging spots mainly benefit those who can afford an expensive electric car. The SP prioritizes better public transport, cycling, and affordable parking for all residents first. The municipality must make mobility fairer, not more unequal.

116610.explanation: People should always be able to choose who cares for or supports them, regardless of the reason. Care is about trust and personal preference. That freedom of choice should be accessible to everyone. The municipality should not restrict this through fixed contracts or commercial interests.

116611.explanation: Good youth care is about quality, not the lowest price. Our health is not a product to profit from. This market mechanism leads to poorer care and longer waiting times. Choose organizations based on expertise, cooperation, and availability. Stable care comes first.

116613.explanation: If extra funds are needed for care, poverty reduction, and affordable housing, a limited property tax increase can be justified. SP Hoorn then opts for a social variant: large companies and expensive properties pay more, while low and middle incomes are spared as much as possible.

116616.explanation: The SP is critical of expanding industrial estates. First, vacant buildings and existing sites must be better utilized. New expansion is only allowed if it is demonstrably necessary for local employment and does not compromise our quality of life and green spaces.

116618.explanation: Municipalities have a legal duty to house status holders, but this does not have to be through priority on the waiting list. The SP wants 50% of new housing to be prioritized for Hoorn housing seekers. The solution for everyone’s housing is more social rental housing and tackling vacancy.

116620.explanation: The SP opposes solar parks on agricultural land. Sustainable energy must be generated without sacrificing fertile land and open landscape. We opt for solar panels on business roofs and existing buildings, protecting food production, nature, and the living environment.

116625.explanation: The SP supports regulated sale of soft drugs through a legal coffee shop. This prevents street dealing, reduces nuisance, and allows better control of age, quality, and health. A small-scale coffee shop can contribute to a safe and livable municipality.

116627.explanation: SP Hoorn is critical of more high residential towers. We solve housing shortages with affordable construction that fits the neighborhood, renovation, and reuse of vacancy. High-rise development can only be small-scale, with support and without harming livability, views, and social cohesion.

116629.explanation: The SP believes every neighborhood should have its own meeting place. Community centers and care community centers bring people together, offer help close to home, and strengthen social cohesion. Therefore, we invest in a neighborhood center or community center function in every neighborhood.

116630.explanation: The SP strives for a car-free city center with affordable parking spaces outside the center and fair rates for residents. Parking fees must not increase further, but making them structurally cheaper is not a goal. Accessibility by public transport and bicycle gets priority.

116631.explanation: The SP wants residents to be able to park their cars in their own neighborhood. In areas with high parking pressure, extra parking spaces or appropriate solutions must be provided. Parking should remain affordable and accessible, especially for residents, caregivers, and healthcare providers.

116633.explanation: The SP wants to redesign the Roode Steen as an inclusive and lively square with space for terraces, (cheese) markets, and events. The JP Coen statue should not remain in the middle of the square but be given an appropriate place by the museum, with an honest and complete explanation of the history.

116634.explanation: The SP wants to keep sports accessible and nearby in every neighborhood. Existing sports locations must be preserved. Football fields are important for health, youth, and club life. Converting these fields solely into parks would unnecessarily reduce sports opportunities.

116635.explanation: The SP strives for a car-free city center. Cars should not dominate squares like Kerkplein. Less traffic means more space for pedestrians, cyclists, greenery, and terraces. This makes the center safer, cleaner, and more attractive for residents and visitors.

116636.explanation: The SP strives for a car-free, accessible city center. Affordable shuttle buses between parking garages and the center make parking outside the center more attractive, help elderly people and visitors, and reduce search traffic and nuisance. This keeps the city center accessible for everyone.

116637.explanation: First, every child must be able to swim and play safely and healthily at the city beach as it is now. The SP wants to stop pollution around Julianapark and protect the Markermeer. Afterwards, we will consider the widely supported need and realization of a new watersports location.

116571.explanation: We want residents to have the chance to make their voices heard more than once every four years. A referendum can be a valuable tool to truly give residents influence on important decisions. A referendum in the Netherlands is advisory and not a binding decision.

116574.explanation: Entrepreneurs know their neighborhood, customers, and opportunities best. Rigid rules help no one. Freedom in opening hours is possible as long as clear agreements exist about livability, safety, and nuisance. The municipality must provide tailor-made solutions: allow space where possible and intervene where necessary.

116575.explanation: We take our responsibility, but oppose the idea that a reception center must necessarily be located within our municipal boundaries. Shelter requires suitable locations, support, and good guidance. This can also be done in good cooperation with neighboring municipalities if that fits better there.

116578.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves requires nuance. We acknowledge that wood-burning can cause nuisance and health risks. At the same time, a general ban is not necessarily needed. Clear rules about where, how, and with what is burned can limit the nuisance.

116583.explanation: Accessibility is important, but financial obligations for entrepreneurs are not always realistic, especially in old buildings. The municipality must support this with advice and tailor-made solutions. Public spaces and municipal buildings must, however, be fully accessible.

116584.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone. In Hoorn, this is already arranged through the HoornPas, with which people with low incomes receive support for sports and culture. We prefer to build on existing, working schemes rather than create new obligations.

116591.explanation: We want residents to feel safe and well-protected. Camera surveillance can contribute to this but must not become a form of symptom management. It does not address the causes of crime, and its effectiveness has not been convincingly demonstrated in all areas.

116593.explanation: Hoorn is currently running a pilot with the baton. We find it wise to first carefully evaluate the results. Safety requires proportional means and a balanced decision, not automatically expanding powers without clear justification.

116594.explanation: Local ties can help improve turnover and livability. At the same time, there must also be room for people from the region and those with a demonstrable connection to Hoorn. We choose a fair and broad approach, not a strict delimitation.

116597.explanation: Converting vacant offices is a quick and sustainable way to realize additional housing. With targeted support, the municipality can enable transformation where the market stalls, without sacrificing new green spaces.

116600.explanation: Remission prevents people with low incomes from falling deeper into trouble. It is an important instrument to provide security and avoid debt. Those who are strongest should bear the heaviest burdens; that is fair and social policy.

116601.explanation: In Hoorn, the income limit is already set high at 120% of the social assistance standard. Instead of generic increases, we choose tailor-made support so that help reaches those who really need it without broadening schemes indiscriminately.

116603.explanation: A basic income pilot can provide valuable insights, provided it is carefully designed and based on proven effective models, such as the Finnish experiment. Less stress and more trust can help people take steps forward again.

116604.explanation: Stimulating electric driving is important, but so is parking pressure. Designating additional charging spots must not come at the expense of available parking spaces. We choose a balanced and demand-driven approach, fitting the neighborhood and need.

116610.explanation: Good care must be available to everyone, regardless of background. Taking personal wishes into account is important, but 'always being able to choose' is not feasible in practice and can lead to exclusion. Professionalism, quality, and accessibility of care come first.

116611.explanation: Quality must come first in youth support. Cheap care that does not work leads to longer trajectories and higher long-term costs. We choose proven effective help, continuity, and cooperation around the child, not the lowest price as the leading criterion.

116613.explanation: An increase in property tax is not an end in itself. Sometimes it may be necessary to maintain facilities, but that requires careful consideration and clear explanation. Increases must remain reasonable and low incomes must be protected.

116616.explanation: Hoorn has limited space. Expanding industrial estates must not come at the cost of green areas, housing, and livability. We first choose to better utilize, restructure, and make existing industrial estates more sustainable before cutting into new space.

116618.explanation: Everyone deserves a fair chance at housing. Priority for one group increases pressure on the already long waiting lists and the feeling of inequality. We choose fair distribution, with attention to urgent cases but without structural priority.

116620.explanation: Hoorn hardly has any agricultural land left. Therefore, a general ban is not logical. We opt for tailor-made solutions: preferably solar panels on roofs, industrial estates, and other suitable locations. Each location must be carefully considered for what fits the environment and landscape.

116625.explanation: Hoorn already has a coffee shop. The policy works and provides clarity and control. Expansion is not obvious and requires careful consideration of safety, nuisance, and enforceability. We choose calm and clear policy.

116627.explanation: High-rise buildings can be a good solution to create more homes without sacrificing extra green space. In the right locations, such as around stations and hubs, taller buildings fit well and contribute to flow, affordability, and more efficient land use.

116629.explanation: Meeting in the neighborhood is important, but not every neighborhood needs a separate community center. Sometimes a shared facility or cooperation with existing places works better. We opt for customized solutions: supporting what works in a neighborhood instead of trying to build the same everywhere.

116630.explanation: Parking fees are an important tool to keep the city center accessible and livable. They encourage turnover and alternatives like cycling or public transport. Structural reductions are not necessary; however, parking policy must be fair, logical, and well explained.

116631.explanation: More street parking is not always the solution and often comes at the expense of greenery and living space. We want to make parking on private property easier and promote (semi)underground parking in new construction. Without raising parking standards.

116633.explanation: The statue is part of Hoorn's history, but that history has multiple facets. We do not choose black-and-white decisions, but context and dialogue. Through proper explanation and education, the square can be a place where space is given to different perspectives.

116634.explanation: Sports and green spaces do not have to exclude each other. Julianapark is already a park, and football club Hollandia has been an important and connecting place there for many years. We prefer to focus on accelerating the greening of the Bangert-Oosterpolder, where much progress can still be made.

116635.explanation: Kerkplein is already largely car-restricted in principle. Completely car-free is not feasible in practice due to residents, loading and unloading, and emergency services. We opt for a workable design with as little traffic as possible, without unrealistic bans.

116636.explanation: Shuttle transport can be a good solution if large parking facilities are created on the city's edges. That situation does not exist now. As long as the city center remains easily accessible, extra transport is not needed. We will review this again if the situation changes.

116637.explanation: The city beach is a place for relaxation and recreation for everyone. A new watersports location does not automatically fit here. We prefer to make better use of existing watersport locations rather than building new ones in this spot.

116571.explanation: This way we get the best possible picture of what the residents want. The pinnacle of democracy.

116575.explanation: Hoorn is full and must remain livable. First, help our own residents with housing and sufficient facilities, like general practitioners.

116578.explanation: This causes air pollution, odors for residents, and irritation of the airways.

116583.explanation: We stand up for people with disabilities, so on this point too, we believe the municipality should financially support if necessary.

116584.explanation: There are also forms of sport and exercise that cost nothing. Additionally, there is already the HoornPas, which gives people with a low income a credit that can be freely spent on, among other things, sports.

116593.explanation: Better for the resilience of enforcement officers (BOAs). Hoorn is already participating in a trial with the short baton.

116594.explanation: The waiting list is enormous. Young adults are forced to stay living with their parents and people wait about fifteen years on the waiting list.

116597.explanation: A good way to somewhat solve the housing problem.

116600.explanation: That would only cause more problems and debts.

116601.explanation: Costs are rising faster than incomes.

116603.explanation: Good to see how this turns out. If the trial fails, we will reconsider.

116604.explanation: These spots are often empty and there is more demand for 'normal' parking spaces.

116610.explanation: There is already a large shortage of people in healthcare. We should be grateful for those who are present, and then you cannot always choose.

116613.explanation: This means a limited group would have to bear the burden.

116616.explanation: There is already much vacancy which, according to H!B, can also be used for housing.

116618.explanation: First solve the waiting lists. Just join the end of the queue.

116620.explanation: Hoorn has hardly any farmland; this is not really applicable.

116625.explanation: There are already several in Hoorn.

116627.explanation: To a limited extent, so the character of Hoorn remains intact. Only in places where this is possible.

116629.explanation: In Hoorn, this is the case almost everywhere.

116630.explanation: It must remain attractive to come to Hoorn and accessible for everyone. Good for retail businesses.

116631.explanation: There is a shortage of parking spaces and only parking spaces are being removed. Older parking spaces are often too narrow for current cars.

116633.explanation: Absolutely. J.P. Coen is part of Hoorn. More information at the statue and education is fine.

116634.explanation: It has already been decided that Hollandia will remain in Julianapark.

116635.explanation: Kerkplein is located in the center and must remain accessible to everyone.

116636.explanation: We would like to replace 'parking garages' with 'parking facilities' outside the city center.

116637.explanation: The city beach is unique and as many people as possible should be attracted there.

116571.explanation: Resident participation is important. A referendum can be useful in specific cases.

116574.explanation: Entrepreneurs can determine their opening hours themselves (within the framework of the law). The municipality does not need to do that.

116575.explanation: HOPE! Is against a refugee reception center in Hoorn. If the COA obliges us to provide emergency shelter, it must be modest, not in a luxury hotel like Van der Valk.

116578.explanation: Fewer fireplaces are better for the environment. However, the question we have is whether local governments should interfere in this.

116583.explanation: Entrepreneurs and the municipality are jointly responsible for the accessibility of public spaces and facilities.

116584.explanation: The sports and culture card must be maintained.

116591.explanation: CCTV surveillance should be applied only if there are no other options to ensure safety.

116593.explanation: By the way, de-escalation must always precede the use of the baton!

116594.explanation: Current residents should not be displaced by people who have no ties to Hoorn.

116600.explanation: For people who truly cannot afford it, exemption must remain possible.

116601.explanation: The current standard of 120 percent of social assistance is sufficient. If necessary, it can be adjusted.

116603.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for wage policy. That is not a task of the municipality.

116604.explanation: There are increasingly more electric cars. The municipality must facilitate that, although a power shortage makes it a challenge.

116610.explanation: People should be able to choose their healthcare providers as much as possible, provided that it is not financially unreasonable.

116611.explanation: Quality and price go hand in hand. Neither should be overlooked.

116613.explanation: Taxes must remain as low as possible. Only if a concrete investment is made would a tax increase be legitimate.

116616.explanation: More business parks are needed. Existing business parks should also be able to densify or expand if reasonably possible.

116618.explanation: Unfortunately, the municipality has the duty to house status holders. However, ways should be explored to do this soberly and efficiently, for example with temporary flexible housing.

116620.explanation: There is little to no agricultural land in Hoorn. Therefore, the advantage is negligible.

116625.explanation: There are already two coffeeshops in Hoorn. That is sufficient.

116627.explanation: High-rise buildings can be useful for densification, for example around the station area. However, ground-level housing remains necessary as well.

116629.explanation: A community center provides a meeting place. Therefore, there must be a community center in every neighborhood.

116630.explanation: The current parking fees are too high. Entrepreneurs and the elderly experience problems because of this. The rate for the second hour must be lowered accordingly.

116631.explanation: There is a shortage of parking spaces in many places.

116633.explanation: The statue of J.P. Coen must remain on the square. However, the entire history should be explained, for example via a QR code and an adapted accompanying text.

116634.explanation: Hollandia remains in Julianapark. However, it should be considered whether the football fields can be used multifunctionally, by schools and other associations.

116635.explanation: The parking spaces on the church square remain necessary for short-term parking. Not everyone can walk long distances to shops and facilities.

116636.explanation: Accessibility to the city center is essential. The elderly cannot always walk long distances. Small buses could provide a solution to improve accessibility for the elderly and entrepreneurs.

116637.explanation: It’s fine that a new water sports location 'must' come, but why under the municipality’s management?

statements.title: The statue of Jan Pieterszoon Coen should remain on the Roode Steen.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Hoorn should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hoorn moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The use of fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Hoorn.

titleParts.text: In Hoorn moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should invest in shuttle buses between the parking garages and the city center.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Hoorn.
There should be more camera surveillance in Hoorn.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every neighborhood in the city should get its own community center.
The Kerkplein should be made car-free.
The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who monitor safety in Hoorn should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Hoorn controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: A park should be created on the site of the football fields in the Julianapark.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Hoorn should run a basic income pilot.

titleParts.text: Hoorn moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: Business parks should be allowed to expand in Hoorn.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Paid parking in the center should be made cheaper.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should build a new watersports facility at the city beach.
